structural_verification.evidence: Original OBReceiveMFR has no session check (line 391). CEViewChange leaves msgs_ce_to_ob UNCHANGED (line 321). Modified adds msg.session = ob_cached_ce_session[m] guard. DownstreamOBReceiveCEViewChange updates cache on VC.

proposal.technique_name: multi-source vector clocking with per-source cursors
proposal.claim: OBReceiveMFR lacks CE session validation, allowing stale MFRs from a rolled-back CE session to be consumed and sequenced as CEFinalization effects in the current OB log
proposal.severity: structural gap in CE-OB data-plane coupling
proposal.properties_note: No existing properties catch this bug. P3 (sequence monotonicity) and P5 (namespace separation) hold because the phantom CEFinalization uses fresh ob_seq/ob_primary values. The gap is semantic: stale cross-session MFR consumption produces a finalization referencing a rolled-back fill. A new property OBCEFinalizationSessionCoherence would be needed to detect this.

analysis.problem: OBReceiveMFR (line 391-405) consumes any message from msgs_ce_to_ob matching msg.market = m, with no session guard. After a CE crash + view change, stale MFRs from the old CE session remain in msgs_ce_to_ob. OB can consume these and produce CEFinalization effects that reference fills which were rolled back during CE's surgical recovery. The CEFinalization gets sequenced into ob_log with the current OB session, creating a phantom finalization.
analysis.root_cause: DownstreamOBReceiveCEViewChange (line 577-585) caches vc.ts into ob_cached_ce_ts but does NOT cache vc.session. OB has no mechanism to know which CE session is authoritative for data-plane messages. The authority timestamp is used only for view change dedup, not for MFR validation.
trace: 1. CE session=1 produces Fill, sends MFR to msgs_ce_to_ob with session=1 (line 244)
2. CE crashes before OB consumes the MFR (CECrash, line 289)
3. CE view change: session=2, some effects rolled back (CEViewChange, line 298)
5. OBReceiveMFR finds msg with market=m in msgs_ce_to_ob (the stale session=1 MFR)
6. No session check: OB produces CEFinalization with seq=ob_seq[m], session=ob_primary[m] (line 395)
7. This CEFinalization references a fill that CE may have rolled back. OB log now contains phantom finalization.
analysis.fix_description: Add ob_cached_ce_session variable (per-market, tracks CE session from view change messages). Guard OBReceiveMFR on msg.session = ob_cached_ce_session[m]. Update DownstreamOBReceiveCEViewChange to cache vc.session alongside vc.ts.
analysis.init_coupling_note: ob_cached_ce_session = [m \in Markets |-> 1] in Init is a required coupling to ce_primary = 1 (line 164). If CE were initialized to any session != 1, OBReceiveMFR would be permanently disabled: no view change would have occurred yet to update ob_cached_ce_session, so the session guard would never match. Both must start at the same value.

diff.description: Three changes to the ORIGINAL spec: (1) add ob_cached_ce_session variable, (2) update DownstreamOBReceiveCEViewChange to cache session, (3) guard OBReceiveMFR on session match

changes.new:
/\ \A m \in Markets : ob_cached_ce_ts[m] \in Nat
    /\ \A m \in Markets : ob_cached_ce_session[m] \in Nat

changes.old:
OBReceiveMFR(m) ==
    /\ ob_alive[m]
    /\ \E msg \in msgs_ce_to_ob :
        /\ msg.market = m
        /\ LET eff == [type |-> "CEFinalization", seq |-> ob_seq[m], session |-> ob_primary[m]]
           IN /\ ob_log' = [ob_log EXCEPT ![m] = @ \union {eff}]
              /\ ob_seq' = [ob_seq EXCEPT ![m] = @ + 1]
              /\ msgs_ob_broadcast' = [msgs_ob_broadcast EXCEPT ![m] = @ \union {eff}]
              /\ msgs_ce_to_ob' = msgs_ce_to_ob \ {msg}
changes.new:
OBReceiveMFR(m) ==
    /\ ob_alive[m]
    /\ \E msg \in msgs_ce_to_ob :
        /\ msg.market = m
        /\ msg.session = ob_cached_ce_session[m]
        /\ LET eff == [type |-> "CEFinalization", seq |-> ob_seq[m], session |-> ob_primary[m]]
           IN /\ ob_log' = [ob_log EXCEPT ![m] = @ \union {eff}]
              /\ ob_seq' = [ob_seq EXCEPT ![m] = @ + 1]
              /\ msgs_ob_broadcast' = [msgs_ob_broadcast EXCEPT ![m] = @ \union {eff}]
              /\ msgs_ce_to_ob' = msgs_ce_to_ob \ {msg}

changes.old:
DownstreamOBReceiveCEViewChange(m) ==
    /\ \E vc \in msgs_view_change :
        /\ vc.svc = "CE"
        /\ vc.ts > ob_cached_ce_ts[m]
        /\ ob_cached_ce_ts' = [ob_cached_ce_ts EXCEPT ![m] = vc.ts]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<ceVars, obVars, omsVars, oms_cached_ce_ts, oms_cached_ob_ts,
                   msgVars,
                   archiverVars, obBackupVars>>
changes.new:
DownstreamOBReceiveCEViewChange(m) ==
    /\ \E vc \in msgs_view_change :
        /\ vc.svc = "CE"
        /\ vc.ts > ob_cached_ce_ts[m]
        /\ ob_cached_ce_ts' = [ob_cached_ce_ts EXCEPT ![m] = vc.ts]
        /\ ob_cached_ce_session' = [ob_cached_ce_session EXCEPT ![m] = vc.session]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<ceVars, obVars, omsVars, oms_cached_ce_ts, oms_cached_ob_ts,
                   msgVars,
                   archiverVars, obBackupVars>>

changes.location: All UNCHANGED clauses that currently mention cacheVars or ob_cached_ce_ts individually must also preserve ob_cached_ce_session. Since ob_cached_ce_session is added to cacheVars, any action using UNCHANGED <<..., cacheVars, ...>> already covers it. Actions that individually list cache vars need ob_cached_ce_session added.
changes.note: DownstreamOMSReceiveCEViewChange (line 596) lists ob_cached_ce_ts individually in UNCHANGED. Must add ob_cached_ce_session there. Similarly DownstreamOMSReceiveOBViewChange (line 616).

proposal.verification_suggestion: Model check with Markets={m1}, MaxSeq=4. Trace: CE produces fill (session=1), CE crashes before OBReceiveMFR, CE view change (session=2), OB attempts OBReceiveMFR. Without fix: OB consumes stale MFR. With fix: OBReceiveMFR is disabled until OB receives CE view change notification updating ob_cached_ce_session to 2, and new session-2 MFR arrives.
proposal.risk_assessment: Low risk. Adding a guard strictly reduces the set of enabled transitions. Existing behaviors where CE session matches are unaffected. The only behavioral change is blocking stale cross-session MFR consumption, which is the intended fix.

review.summary: Both required documentation fixes (J1, J2) are confirmed applied. properties_affected is [] with a clear note explaining why P3/P5/P10 are not violated. The Init coupling between ob_cached_ce_session=1 and ce_primary=1 is documented in init_coupling_note. The diff is unchanged from the version the reviewer already approved structurally (R4, R5 confirmed sound). No further changes required.

J1_applied.evidence: properties_affected = [] at line 9. properties_note explains that P3/P5/P10 hold because phantom CEFinalization uses fresh ob_seq/ob_primary values, and names a new property OBCEFinalizationSessionCoherence that would be needed to detect the gap.

J2_applied.evidence: init_coupling_note at line 24 explicitly states: ob_cached_ce_session = [m \in Markets |-> 1] is a required coupling to ce_primary = 1, and explains the deadlock scenario if they diverge.

review.diff_assessment: unchanged from prior review. Structural soundness previously confirmed: variable addition, cacheVars update, TypeInvariant extension, Init initialization, OBReceiveMFR session guard, DownstreamOBReceiveCEViewChange session caching, and UNCHANGED clause patches for DownstreamOMSReceiveCEViewChange and DownstreamOMSReceiveOBViewChange are all correct.

judgment.summary: Both required documentation fixes applied. Bug is real, fix is structurally sound, documentation is now accurate. Proposal advances.

basis.bug_validity: confirmed. CEViewChange UNCHANGED msgs_ce_to_ob (line 322), OBReceiveMFR no session guard (line 391). Stale cross-session MFRs are consumable, producing phantom CEFinalizations.
basis.fix_soundness: confirmed. Session guard msg.session = ob_cached_ce_session[m] closes the gap. DownstreamOBReceiveCEViewChange correctly caches vc.session. UNCHANGED coverage complete.
basis.j1_resolved: properties_affected = []. properties_note accurately explains semantic gap and names a candidate new property.
basis.j2_resolved: init_coupling_note documents the ob_cached_ce_session=1 / ce_primary=1 invariant and the deadlock consequence of divergence.
